name: Taiwan Province (popularly attributed)

colors.symbolism: the field of the provincial emblem
the disc behind the provincial emblem
the silhouette map of Taiwan, the inscription, and the canton of the ROC flag within the emblem
the canton of the ROC flag within the emblem
the plum-blossom petals and the twelve-rayed sun in the ROC canton
outlines of the emblem and inscription

description: A yellow field charged with the seal of the Taiwan Provincial Government: a light-blue disc framed by a yellow ring, bearing a stylised red silhouette of the island of Taiwan overlaid with a five-petalled plum blossom (the national flower of the Republic of China), with a small ROC national flag canton above and the red four-character inscription 臺灣省政府 ('Taiwan Provincial Government') below.
history: Despite being commonly indexed on Wikipedia and similar references as the 'Flag of Taiwan Province' — and accordingly mapped to the ISO 3166-2 code CN-TW (the People's Republic of China's claimed subdivision) — this design is in fact the seal-flag of the Republic of China's *own* Taiwan Provincial Government, which administered most of the island between 1947 and 1998. The provincial government was streamlined in December 1998 (its functions transferred to the Executive Yuan) and formally abolished on 1 July 2018, since which point no provincial flag has been in active use. The People's Republic of China, which claims Taiwan as its 23rd province, has *not* published any flag for that claimed province; vexillological references (FOTW, Wikipedia 'Taiwan Province (PRC)') confirm there is no PRC-published artifact. The file is included under `CN-TW` to capture the popular attribution and the only artifact actually associated with the name 'Flag of Taiwan Province'; the canonical Taiwanese flag is the national flag of the Republic of China, recorded at `TW`.

features.symbolism: the yellow field of the provincial standard

features.role: Seal of the Taiwan Provincial Government — light-blue disc with red Taiwan-island silhouette, five-petalled plum blossom (national flower), inset ROC flag and the inscription 臺灣省政府
features.symbolism: marks the provincial government as a subordinate organ of the Republic of China; the plum blossom is the ROC national flower
